title: Weekly Insights Review
summary:
Read here everything you need to know


Latest Trends
Owners of ships burning methane-based fuels such as LNG are unlikely to invest in technologies to reduce methane emissions without strong incentives or regulatory requirements, according to a study by the Mærsk Mc-Kinney Møller Center for Zero Carbon Shipping (MMMCZCS). Read More

Τhe worst bond market volatility in decades
How is it that within the space of a few weeks Kwasi Kwarteng was as unceremoniously sacked as Greek Gianis Varoufakis in 2015, and Liz Truss's position is as, if not more, precarious than Silvio Berlusconi's in 2012? The answer is really not that surprising: It's Quantitative Tightening meeting political moral hazard.

children.title: Latest Trends
children.summary: Owners of ships burning methane-based fuels such as LNG are unlikely to invest in technologies to reduce methane emissions without strong incentives or regulatory requirements, according to a study by the Mærsk Mc-Kinney Møller Center for Zero Carbon Shipping (MMMCZCS).

The answer is really not that surprising: It's Quantitative Tightening meeting political moral hazard.

children.title: Crude prices rose on the news of OPEC's decision
section_path: Crude prices rose on the news of OPEC's decision
children.section_path_text: Crude prices rose on the news of OPEC's decision
children.summary: Last week, OPEC+ agreed to make production cuts of 2 mbd for the next 14 months from November 2022 to December 2023, representing a 2% reduction in global oil supply.

children.title: 487.4 million tons of exports are now expected
section_path: 487.4 million tons of exports are now expected
children.section_path_text: 487.4 million tons of exports are now expected
children.summary:
The United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) has released their latest global grain export forecast for the 2022/23 season and has again reduced its expectations. Read More

children.section_path_text: Global crude inventories have been volatile in 2022
